heading: Report on protocols for certain intelligence community employees and dependents
label: 3334k Report on protocols for certain intelligence community employees and dependents
notes: Standard Guidelines for Intelligence Community To Report and Document Anomalous Health Incidents, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–60, div. F, title LXVIII, § 6806139 Stat. 1657Dec. 18, 2025“(a).—Standard GuidelinesNot later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Director of National Intelligence shall, in coordination with such heads of elements of the intelligence community as the Director considers appropriate, develop and issue standard guidelines for personnel of the intelligence community to report and properly document anomalous health incidents.Dec. 18, 2025“(b).—Conformity With Department of Defense GuidelinesIn developing the standard guidelines required by subsection (a), the Director shall ensure that such standard guidelines are as similar as practicable to guidelines issued by the Secretary of Defense for personnel of the Department of Defense to report and properly document anomalous health incidents.“(c).—SubmissionNot later than 10 days after the date on which the Director issues the standard guidelines required by subsection (a), the Director shall submit to the congressional intelligence committees the standard guidelines, including a statement describing the implementation of such standard guidelines, how the standard guidelines differ from those issued by the Secretary, and the justifications for such differences.”[For definitions of “intelligence community” and “congressional intelligence committees” as used in , set out above, see , set out as a note under .]section 6806 of Pub. L. 119–60section 6002 of Pub. L. 119–60section 3003 of this title

DefinitionsFor definitions of “congressional intelligence committees” and “intelligence community” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of div. X of Pub. L. 117–103section 3003 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Beginning not later than 180 days after , the President shall develop, for uniform implementation across the elements of the intelligence community, each of the protocols described in subsections (c) through (f). Such protocols shall be subject to review and revision on a periodic basis, and any implementation of such protocols shall be conducted in accordance with applicable laws and current clinical and professional practices of the interagency medical community.March 15, 2022

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: b Privacy
children.text: No data collected pursuant to any protocol under this section may be used for research or analytical purposes without the written consent of the individual from whom such data was collected with respect to such use.

children.heading: Protocol on baseline medical testing
children.label: c Protocol on baseline medical testing

children.text: standard lab collection and testing of relevant biofluids;
the conduct of relevant visual and auditory examinations;
the conduct of Acquired Brain Injury Tool assessments, or other relevant assessments for balance, eye motion, and cognition;
the assessment of relevant medical histories; and
the conduct of any other standard relevant medical or neurological examinations, testing, or assessments.

children.heading: Protocols on post-incident medical testing
children.label: d Protocols on post-incident medical testing

children.text: a protocol that sets forth elements, similar to the elements described in subsection (c), of such testing;
a protocol pertaining to the voluntary testing and treatment for victims of anomalous health incidents who are children;

a protocol for ensuring that all victims of anomalous health incidents receive access to prompt and consistent medical treatment, including from medical professionals holding appropriate security clearances and medical professionals with expertise in child care;
a protocol for ensuring that all victims of anomalous health incidents are offered options for psychological treatment for the effects of such incidents; and

a protocol for ensuring that any testing, evaluation, or collection of biofluids or other samples following a reported anomalous health incident may be compared against the baseline for the victim of the anomalous health incident, to the extent the individual participated in the baseline medical testing, consistent with subsections (b) and (c).

children.heading: Protocol on information collection, storage, and safeguarding
children.label: e Protocol on information collection, storage, and safeguarding
children.text: The protocol described in this subsection is a protocol for the collection, storage, and safeguarding of information acquired as a result of the protocols described in subsections (c) and (d).

children.heading: Protocol on reporting mechanisms
children.label: f Protocol on reporting mechanisms
children.text: The protocol described in this subsection is a protocol for the reporting of matters relating to anomalous health incidents by covered employees, covered individuals, and the dependents of covered employees, including the development of a system for the adjudication of complaints regarding medical treatment received by such covered employees, covered individuals, and dependents of covered employees.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 1 Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Director of National Intelligence shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the protocols described in subsections (c) through (f).March 15, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: A copy of each protocol under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: Any interagency agreements, authorities, or policies required to effectively implement the protocols under this section.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any new facilities, medical equipment, tools, training, or other resources required to effectively implement such protocols.

children.label: C
children.text: A timeline for the implementation of the protocols under this section, including a proposal for the prioritization of implementation with respect to various categories of covered employees and the dependents of covered employees.

children.heading: Briefing
children.label: 3 Briefing
children.text: Not later than 60 days following the date of submission of the report under paragraph (1), and biannually thereafter, the Director shall provide to the appropriate congressional committees a briefing regarding the implementation of the protocols under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the congressional intelligence committees; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committees on Armed Services of the House of Representatives and the Senate.

children.heading: Covered employee
children.label: 2 Covered employee
children.text: The term “covered employee” means an individual who is an employee, assignee, or detailee of an element of the intelligence community.

children.heading: Covered individual
children.label: 3 Covered individual
children.text: The term “covered individual” means a contractor to an element of the intelligence community.

children.heading: Dependent of a covered employee
children.label: 4 Dependent of a covered employee
children.text: The term “dependent of a covered employee” means, with respect to a covered employee, a family member (including a child), as defined by the Director of National Intelligence.

children.heading: Victim of an anomalous health incident
children.label: 5 Victim of an anomalous health incident
children.text: The term “victim of an anomalous health incident” means a covered employee, covered individual, or dependent of a covered employee, who is, or is suspected to have been, affected by an anomalous health incident.
